tiktok.caption: You saved $34 at checkout with a store card signup. That yes is going to cost you $2,800. Here's why the math never works in your favor 🚨

youtube_short.title: That Store Card Discount Is a $2,800 Debt Trap
title_alternatives: Why You Should Never Say Yes to a Store Credit Card
Store Cards Charge 28.9% — Here's What That Actually Costs You
youtube_short.description: Store credit cards charge an average of 28.9% APR — nearly 10 points higher than a regular card. Forty-two percent of people who open them end up carrying a balance. A $1,200 balance at that rate takes two years to pay off, and by the time you're done, you've paid $2,800 for $1,200 worth of stuff. And every time you say yes at a checkout register, you take a hard inquiry that can drop your credit score 5 to 10 points. Three store cards in a year? That's potentially 30 points gone. The fix is simple: say no, use a flat 2% cashback card instead. #personalfinance #creditcards #moneytips #debtfree #storecards
